message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: Wally's Desert Turtle (Rancho Mirage)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 515 reviews; Tripadvisor 357 reviews (#3 of 80 in Rancho Mirage); OpenTable cited as 4.6 across ~4,266 diner reviews. Google review count [UNVERIFIED] — not directly fetched.
- Sentiment: Mostly positive — strong recurring praise for service, nostalgia/Rat Pack ambiance, Dover sole, rack of lamb, and soufflés; minority of reviews note high prices and occasional pacing/wait issues.
- Longevity: Operating since 1978 — 48 years. Continuous family ownership: founder Wally Botello, then son Michael Botello (after Wally's passing in 1985), and now third-generation owner Madalyn Botello (Cornell hospitality alumna).
- Credentials: ["Wine Spectator Award of Excellence (current as of 2025)","AAA Four Diamond Award (long-running; site/press claim continuous since 1993 — [UNVERIFIED] for the start-year specifically)","DiRoNA Award (Distinguished Restaurants of North America) — cited in press [UNVERIFIED] for current status","Mobil Travel Four Star Award — cited in press [UNVERIFIED] for current/historic status","Featured on Palm Springs Life 'oldest restaurants' / 'legends of fine dining' lists","Featured on Visit Greater Palm Springs and OpenTable historic-restaurants lists"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces strongly for: 'best fine dining Rancho Mirage', 'romantic / date night Rancho Mirage', 'historic restaurants Greater Palm Springs', 'continental restaurants Palm Springs / Palm Desert cities'. Does NOT reliably surface for breakfast queries (dinner-only, seasonal), and is overshadowed by Melvyn's, Le Vallauris, and Johannes on broad 'continental Palm Springs' queries that center Palm Springs proper.
- Site structured data: Site (Squarespace) presents clean human-readable hours, menu sections (Dinner, Desserts, Bar & Happy Hour, Wine, Restaurant Week, Private Dining), address, phone, and social links. JSON-LD / schema.org Restaurant markup was not confirmed in fetch — agent-readable structured data is [UNVERIFIED] and likely thin beyond Squarespace defaults. Seasonal-closure messaging is prominent but not expressed as machine-readable openingHoursSpecification.
- Visibility gap score: 4
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's the history of Wally's Desert Turtle and the Botello family:

**Founding and History:**
Wally's Desert Turtle in Rancho Mirage was established in 1978 by Mr. Wally Botello, the founder of the famed fine-dining Velvet Turtle chain. With a little help from renowned designer Steven Chase and the restaurant's original French chef, Jean-Louis Jalouneix, Botello created a five-star dining experience right in the heart of Rancho Mirage.

**The Velvet Turtle Background:**
The Velvet Turtle chain started in Menlo Park, California in the 1960s and once numbered more than 20 locations in California, Arizona and Washington. The chain was bought out by Marriott in 1986 and subsequently closed.

**Family Legacy:**
After Wally's passing in 1985, his son Michael Botello took the reins, nurturing the restaurant into a treasured hidden gem within the valley. Third-generation owner Madalyn Botello took the restaurant over from her father in 2020. For over forty-five years, Wally's Desert Turtle has been honored to host a distinguished array of celebrities, world dignitaries, and esteemed guests, including Bob and Dolores Hope, Frank Sinatra, President Gerald R. and Mrs. Betty Ford, Benjamin Netanyahu, and Tom Hanks.

## Top Fine Dining Options

According to Yelp's latest update in November 2025, top fine dining options include The Edge Steakhouse, Foxy's Kitchen + Bar, Morgan's in the Desert, Willies Modern Fare, Wally's Desert Turtle, Eddie V's Prime Seafood, and Clandestino.

### Highlighted Favorites:

**The Edge Steakhouse** - Perched cliffside atop the glittering lights of Coachella Valley, this elegant steakhouse at The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage was created by award-winning Chef Darryl Boyd and serves only the finest cuts of American Wagyu, all dry-aged in house.

**Wally's Desert Turtle** - Founded in 1978 by acclaimed restaurateur Wally Botello, this is a bona fide piece of Rancho Mirage history and is revered as the origin of fine dining in the desert.

**Willie's Modern Fare** - This restaurant pairs five-star hospitality with fine dining elegance, offering classic cuisine with a modern twist, complemented by inventive cocktails and a thoughtfully curated wine list.

**The Barn Kitchen** - This Michelin Guide Recommended Restaurant for 2022, 2023, 2024 and 2025 offers a refined take on rustic American cuisine.

**Oceana** - A new seafood concept that expanded to Rancho Mirage in 2025, with fan favorites including pulpo bites, lobster guacamole, and ahi tuna nachos, and a cocktail list featuring rare tequilas and small-batch mezcals.

**Wine Spectator** - The page displays a Wine Spectator badge linking to the restaurant's profile, though no specific award year or honor is detailed in the content provided.

No other awards (AAA Four Diamond, DiRoNA, or Mobil Travel) are mentioned in this webpage.

## Founding and Family Ownership

**Founding Year:** The restaurant was established when Wally Botello relocated to the Coachella Valley and created his "final masterpiece" in Rancho Mirage. The exact founding date is not specified in this content.

**Ownership Lineage:**
- **First Generation:** Wally Botello (founder; passed away in 1985)
- **Second Generation:** Michael Botello (Wally's son; took over after 1985)
- **Third Generation:** Madalyn Botello (Michael's daughter; currently leading the restaurant)

**Note:** Cornell is not mentioned anywhere in the provided webpage content.

# Structured Data Analysis: Wally's Desert Turtle

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Madalyn Botello, Cornell hospitality, and Wally's Desert Turtle:

Madalyn Botello is a third-generation owner of Wally's Desert Turtle. She attended Cornell University's Hotel School and graduated in 2017 with her Bachelor of Science in Hospitality.

Her grandfather started a chain of restaurants called The Velvet Turtles and sold those restaurants to open his last restaurant, Wally's Desert Turtle, which is currently run by herself and her father, Michael Botello. Upon graduating from Cornell, she moved to Washington D.C and furthered her education with The Four Seasons Management in Training Program, then worked for The Four Seasons as a Hotel Assistant Manager for about four years.

As a Cornell University hospitality school alum, Botello has enlivened offerings with her fresh perspective — bringing in boutique wines, elevating the outdoor scene, and adding creative twists to the nightly live music. Wally's Desert Turtle has been in the desert for 43 years and counting, serving modern Californian cuisine with a French influence as a farm-to-table restaurant serving only the highest quality, organic ingredients.

Based on the search results, I found comprehensive information about Wally's Desert Turtle and its awards:

## Wally's Desert Turtle Awards

Wally's Desert Turtle is a proud recipient of the prestigious Mobil Travel Four Star Award, AAA Four Diamond Award and the DiRoNA Award.

The restaurant is located in Rancho Mirage and has maintained its AAA Four Diamond designation since 1993.

## About the Restaurant

This magnificent restaurant was built by Wally Botello, the founder of the famed Velvet Turtle chain, and is now owned and operated by his son Michael. The restaurant features beveled mirrored ceilings, Peruvian artifacts, and hand-painted murals that create a warm and elegant ambiance.

The talented chefs create excellently prepared and artistically presented contemporary California cuisine infused with French flavors, resulting in dishes such as roasted free-range chicken with mushroom pudding, skillet-sautéed New York steak with black truffle creamed spinach and roasted rack of lamb.

Based on the search results, here are some excellent continental restaurants in Palm Springs perfect for a date night:

## Top Continental Restaurants for Date Night:

**Miro's Restaurant**
Celebrated as the best Mediterranean-Continental fine dining experience in the area, this restaurant promises an unforgettable culinary journey. Guests rave about the "amazing service" and "fabulous food," with dishes like the Mediterranean chicken and branzino frequently highlighted as standout options. The ambiance is described as "old world and very refined," making it perfect for both intimate dinners and special occasions.

**Zin American Bistro**
Zin American Bistro stands out as a beacon of Continental cuisine, offering a delightful blend of classic and creative dishes. With over 20 years of serving locals and visitors alike, the restaurant has perfected the art of delivering "consistently enchanting" dining experiences. The Wine Spectator Award of Excellence is a testament to the exceptional wine pairings that complement the meals.

**Johannes**
Johannes in downtown Palm Springs is a beacon of Contemporary European cuisine, seamlessly blending authenticity and creativity. Chef Johannes Bacher, renowned for his global modern European style, offers dishes that are both innovative and rooted in his Austrian heritage.

**FARM**
FARM is a traditional Provencial-style restaurant in downtown Palm Springs in the historic Plaza, tucked away from traffic. Chef has taken care to create a menu comprised of traditional European dishes, brimming with locally sourced produce and artisanal meats. In true Provencial-style, the prix fixe dinner menu is served at a pace so you may enjoy your evening with us. Enjoy a meal in our outdoor setting and take in the ambiance of being out of the city and transported magically to the South of France.

These restaurants offer the sophisticated European-inspired cuisine and romantic atmospheres perfect for a memorable date night in Palm Springs.

content.text: I have what I need to deliver a verdict.

confirms: Tripadvisor: 357 reviews, ranked #3 of 80 in Rancho Mirage (confirmed via direct fetch of Tripadvisor page; rating 4.3).
Yelp review volume: 515 reviews (confirmed via Yelp listing title 'Updated May 2026 - 468 Photos & 515 Reviews').
OpenTable: 4.6 rating across ~4,266 diner reviews (confirmed via search snippet citing OpenTable directly).
Founded 1978 by Wally Botello (founder of the Velvet Turtle chain); confirmed across multiple sources including localemagazine, Palm Springs Life, and the official site.
Family ownership lineage: Wally (d.1985) -> Michael Botello -> Madalyn Botello (third generation). Confirmed via wallysdesertturtle.com/about and Palm Springs Life.
Madalyn Botello is a Cornell hospitality alumna — Cornell University Hotel School, BS Hospitality, class of 2017. Confirmed via Palm Springs Life feature.

Surfaces strongly on 'best fine dining Rancho Mirage' queries — appears on Yelp top 10 fine dining, OpenTable's '10 Best Fine Dining Restaurants in Rancho Mirage,' Palm Springs Life '6 Rancho Mirage Restaurants Worth a Reservation,' and Locale Magazine 'date night Rancho Mirage' lists.
Sentiment profile (service, Rat Pack/nostalgia, Dover sole, rack of lamb, soufflés) matches what appears in cited reviews; soufflés and service are repeatedly highlighted in OpenTable summary.

Squarespace site lacks JSON-LD / schema.org Restaurant structured data. Independently confirmed via fetch — only plain HTML address, tel: link, and human-readable seasonal-closure text. No openingHoursSpecification, no menu schema.
contradicts: Seasonal status as currently presented on the homepage: site explicitly says 'CLOSED FOR THE SEASON. We will reopen on October 9, 2026.' This is consistent with the 'seasonal' note in the assessment but stronger — the homepage is currently a closure notice, which materially affects current visibility/convertibility. The brief should reflect that the site at audit time is in closed-for-season state.

Wine Spectator Award of Excellence 'current as of 2025' — site shows a Wine Spectator badge but the about page does not specify award year. The claim is plausible but the year-currency wording overstates what is directly visible on the site.
flagged_as_unverified: AAA Four Diamond continuous since 1993 — the start-year 1993 is repeated in a third-party AAA.com travel listing (confirms current AAA Four Diamond), but 'continuous since 1993' was not independently corroborated against an AAA historical list (the 2025 PDF could not be parsed).
DiRoNA Award current status — cited in third-party listings (e.g., AAA.com travel page) as a recipient, but DiRoNA's own current membership roster was not independently checked.

Mobil Travel Four Star Award — cited in third-party listing alongside AAA/DiRoNA; Mobil Travel Guide rebranded to Forbes Travel Guide in 2009, so any 'current' Mobil Four Star claim is anachronistic and should be reframed as historic.
Google review count — not fetched (consistent with original [UNVERIFIED] flag).

Palm Springs Life 'oldest restaurants' / 'legends of fine dining' specific list inclusion — Palm Springs Life features on the restaurant are confirmed, but the specific list titles cited were not directly enumerated.
Visit Greater Palm Springs and OpenTable historic-restaurants list inclusion — Visit GPS surfaces Rancho Mirage restaurant directory but specific historic-list membership not directly confirmed.
input.notes: Core review-volume and ranking claims hold up (Yelp 515, Tripadvisor 357 #3 of 80, OpenTable 4.6/~4266). Lineage and Cornell credential for Madalyn Botello verified. Structured-data assessment verified — Squarespace site has no JSON-LD Restaurant markup, only plain HTML. Two cautions for the brief: (1) the homepage is currently displaying a hard 'closed for season, reopen Oct 9, 2026' notice, which is a current-state visibility issue the brief should call out explicitly; (2) the 'Mobil Travel Four Star' credential is anachronistic — Mobil rebranded to Forbes Travel Guide in 2009, so listing 'Mobil' as a present credential is misleading and should be reframed as historic. AAA Four Diamond 'since 1993' could not be independently sourced beyond third-party repetition. Visibility gap score of 4 and 'convertible' tier are reasonable given strong third-party surfacing but thin first-party structured data and seasonal-closure homepage.
